revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:富士浅間神社 (藤岡市)]]


{{infobox religious building
| name    = {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|en|Fuji Sengen Shrine|qid=Q11456094|lt=Fuji Sengen Shrine|1=Fuji Sengen Shrine}}
| native_name = 富士浅間神社
| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}
| image = File:GunmakenFijiokashi-FujiSengenjinja-01.jpg|size=270px
| address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|qid=Q596486|lt=Fujioka City|1=Fujioka, Gunma}} 1152番地
| 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|15|1.20|N|139|4|26.79|E|scale:5000}}
| 祭神 = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
| 社格 = 旧村社
| 創建 = Tradition: during the {{ill|Bun'ō era|ja|文応年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'ō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1260–1261 AD)
| 本殿 =
| 例祭 =
| location = {{ill|Fujioka|qid=Q596486|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}
| coordinates = {{Coord|36.2503333|N|139.0741083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
}}

'''Fuji Sengen Shrine''' (富士浅間神社, ふじせんげんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Fujioka City, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|qid=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrines|1=Sengen shrine}}, enshrining primarily {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}, and is believed to bestow blessings for safe childbirth and child-rearing.

== Overview ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. According to Takai Otofu's "Chōōte Kan" during the {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era|1=Tenpō}}, it is said that during the time of {{ill|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|qid=Q311478ja|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|qid=Q31147812=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} when he headed to Kamakura from the east in the {{ill|Bun'ō era|ja|文応年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'ō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1260–1261 AD), he enshrined the sacred scriptures on an eight-pole pillar at Tokigaoka Saketuzuka, thereby inviting the divine spirit of {{ill|Fuji Sengen|ja|富士仙元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Sengen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This act is claimed to be the origin of the place name "Fujioka" (later becoming Fujioka City). However, because the Bun'ō period lasted for less than one year and because Nichiren, who authored the {{ill|Risshō Ankokuron|en|Risshō Ankokuron|qid=Q11598026|lt=Risshō Ankoku Ron|1=Risshō Ankokuron}}, was attacked at his hermitage and subsequently traveled to Kazusa, it is doubted that he actually visited Fujioka.<ref>[https://azumashrine.wixsite.com/website-1 由緒] - Fuji Sengen Shrine</ref><ref>[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/2/3/jyuyoubunkazai/1059.html Fuji Sengen Shrine] - Fujioka City Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://maruchiba.jp/event/detail_12983.html Fuji Sengen Shrine] - Gunma Prefecture Tourism and Local Products Association</ref>

According to the "Fuji-Kengen Engi-sho" of the {{ill|Kyōwa|en|Kyōwa|qid=Q960763|lt=Kyōwa era|1=Kyōwa}} 3rd year (1570 AD, also known as the first year of {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|qid=Q1145064|lt=Genkō|1=Genki (era)}}), {{ill|Ashida Nobumori|ja|芦田右衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashida Nobumori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the shrine building and was appointed to administer a separate Hōin (清源法印) to vouchsafe the shrine lands. During the Edo Period, the shrine building was rebuilt in both the first year of the {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|qid=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} (1804 AD) and the 7th year of the {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|qid=Q1327259|lt=Ansei era|1=Ansei}} (1860 AD). In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1870 AD), the shrine was renamed "Tokaoka Shrine," but on March 19, {{ill|1903|en|1903|qid=Q2045|lt=Meiji 36|1=1903}} (1903 AD) it was restored to the name "Fuji Sengen Shrine." In December of Meiji 39 (1906 AD), it was designated as a provider of sacred offerings (神饌幣帛料供進社). A mysterious fire in Showa 40 (1965 AD) on May 24 destroyed the shrine, and it was rebuilt in Showa 44 (1969 AD).<ref>{{Sfn|藤岡町史編纂委員会|1957|pp=1455–1457}}</ref><ref>{{Sfn|多野藤岡地方誌編集委員会|1976|pp=20–21}}</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}{{Sfn|藤岡町史編纂委員会|1957|pp=1455–1457}}{{Sfn|多野藤岡地方誌編集委員会|1976|pp=20–21}}

== Cultural properties, etc. ==
* Sword (Bizen Sukebukuro){{Sfn|多野藤岡地方誌編集委員会|1976|pp=20–21}}

=== Fujioka City-Designated Important Cultural Properties ===
* Fuji Sengen Shrine Festival Emaki (painted scroll) by {{ill|Kikukawa Eizan|en|Kikukawa Eizan|qid=Q947896|lt=Kikukawa Eizan|1=Kikukawa Eizan}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=富士浅間神社祭礼絵巻|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/2/3/jyuyoubunkazai/1059.html|publisher={{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|qid=Q596486|lt=Fujioka City|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}|accessdate=2024-08-26|language=ja}}</ref>
* Four sets of Suited Armor (Kōchū 4 Shurui)<ref>{{Cite web|title=富士浅間神社具足4種{{!}}藤岡市|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/2/3/jyuyoubunkazai/1059.html|publisher={{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|qid=Q596486|lt=Fujioka City|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}|accessdate=2024-08-26|language=ja}}</ref>

== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Gunma Prefecture Route 30, Jiao Fujioka Line|ja|群馬県道30号寺尾藤岡線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture Route 30, Jiao Fujioka Line|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 30|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11609806}}
* {{ill|Fujioka City Second Elementary School|ja|藤岡市立藤岡第二小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujioka City Second Elementary School|1=Fujioka City Second Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623881}}

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Bibliography ==
